book: English Vocabulary in Use Pre-intermediate and Intermediate

units.title: Learning vocabulary
units.topic: Study skills for learning and revising vocabulary

words.description: Something you do often and usually in the same way
words.example: It's a good idea to have a routine when you use this book.

words.description: Not less than a particular amount
words.example: Study a new unit for at least 30-45 minutes.

words.description: Happening or done once a week
words.example: A weekly routine when you study a new unit.

words.description: Happening or done every day
words.example: A daily routine when you revise that unit.

words.description: To study something again that you have already learned
words.example: You may only need to revise for five or ten minutes each time.

words.description: Doing things energetically rather than passively
words.example: When you are studying a unit for the first time, you need to be active.

words.description: To speak something so you can hear it
words.example: With a new word or phrase, say it aloud.

words.description: In your head, not speaking out loud
words.example: Repeat it to yourself silently.

words.description: A pen used to mark important text in bright colour
words.example: Use a highlighter pen to mark words you think are important.

words.description: To remove pencil marks using a rubber/eraser
words.example: You can rub them out (using a rubber) and do them again later.

words.description: A small object used to remove pencil marks
words.example: You can rub them out using a rubber.

words.description: To check what you remember without looking at the answer
words.example: Test yourself, e.g. look at a word and cover the meaning.

units.title: Keeping a vocabulary notebook
units.topic: Strategies for recording new vocabulary in a notebook

words.description: To record information in writing
words.example: I write down new English words and phrases in my notebook.

words.description: The conversion of words from one language to another
words.example: Next to each word I write a Spanish translation.

words.description: A state of being untidy or disorganised
words.example: My notebook is a mess. [nothing is in a good order]

words.description: To create a picture using a pen or pencil
words.example: I like to draw pictures.

words.description: To write down something so you remember it
words.example: I sometimes make a note of new words in my notebook.

words.description: To not remember something
words.example: I often forget. [don't remember]

words.description: Organised according to subject category
words.example: I try and list words by topic, so I put all the animals together.

words.description: To write down information briefly
words.example: I note down new words and phrases.

words.description: A description of what something means
words.example: I write an explanation in English if it is not difficult.

words.description: A piece of advice to help you with something
words.example: A tip is a piece of advice to help you.

words.description: To put things together without organisation, causing confusion
words.example: Don't mix words from different topics up.

words.description: Needed; required
words.example: Write a translation if it is necessary.

words.description: Not needed; not required
words.example: Don't write things that are unnecessary.

words.description: A word with the same or similar meaning to another word
words.example: awful (adjective) = terrible (synonym: dreadful)

words.description: Words that are commonly used together
words.example: Example sentences help you with word partners (collocations).

units.title: Using a dictionary
units.topic: How to use dictionaries effectively and common defining words

words.description: Using or able to use two languages
words.example: A bilingual dictionary is easy for you to understand.

words.description: To find a word's meaning in a dictionary
words.example: If you look up a word using the dictionary...

words.description: An explanation of the meaning of a word
words.example: A definition explains the meaning.

words.description: Symbols used to show the pronunciation of a word
words.example: Pronunciation using phonemic symbols.

words.description: A grammatical category of a word, e.g. noun, verb, adjective
words.example: [U] tells you that 'fun' is a noun.

words.description: To give something more attention and importance
words.example: My teacher has always emphasised the importance of writing down new words.

words.description: Having a relationship with someone or something
words.example: Musical is connected with / related to music.

words.description: How much there is of something
words.example: ¬£5 million is a large amount of money.

words.description: Done by the government or someone in authority
words.example: A passport is an official document.

words.description: To do or say things in a particular way
words.example: People can behave strangely when they're nervous.

words.description: A letter in a word that is not pronounced
words.example: In the word island, the letter 's' is silent.

units.title: English language words
units.topic: Grammar terms: parts of speech, pronunciation and punctuation

words.description: A word used in place of a noun, e.g. I, you, he, she, it
words.example: I is a pronoun.

words.description: A word for a person, place or thing
words.example: Chair, window, morning and music are all nouns.

words.description: A word describing an action or state
words.example: Have, sit and listen are verbs.

words.description: A word that describes a noun
words.example: Brown and leather are adjectives.

words.description: A word that describes a verb, adjective or another adverb
words.example: Often is an adverb.

words.description: A word showing the relationship between a noun and other words, e.g. in, at, on
words.example: By and to are prepositions.

words.description: The word 'the', used for specific things
words.example: The is a definite article.

words.description: The word 'a' or 'an', used for non-specific things
words.example: A is an indefinite article.

words.description: A word that connects clauses or sentences, e.g. and, but, so
words.example: And is a conjunction or link word.

words.description: A noun that can be counted and has a plural form
words.example: Books, apples, chairs are countable nouns.

words.description: A noun that cannot be counted and has no plural form
words.example: Information, advice are uncountable nouns.

words.description: Following the standard grammar pattern, e.g. for past tense -ed
words.example: Work, live are regular verbs.

words.description: Not following the standard grammar pattern
words.example: Go/went, take/took are irregular verbs.

words.description: A verb combined with a preposition or adverb to make a new meaning
words.example: Take something off, wake up are phrasal verbs.

words.description: Letters added to the beginning of a word that change its meaning
words.example: In uncomfortable, un- is a prefix.

words.description: Letters added to the end of a word that change its meaning or form
words.example: -able is a suffix.

words.description: A unit of pronunciation with one vowel sound
words.example: Book has one syllable, before has two syllables.

words.description: The emphasis placed on a syllable when pronouncing a word
words.example: It is important to know which syllable to stress.

words.description: An uppercase letter used at the beginning of sentences and names
words.example: Every sentence must begin with a capital letter.

words.description: The punctuation mark '.' used at the end of a sentence
words.example: Every sentence must end with a full stop.

words.description: The punctuation mark ',' used to show a pause in a sentence
words.example: Some sentences have a comma.

words.description: The punctuation mark '?' used at the end of a question
words.example: A question must end with a question mark.

units.title: Country, nationality and language
units.topic: Countries, nationalities, languages and parts of the world

words.description: To be originally from a particular country
words.example: I come from Argentina, so I'm Argentinian.

words.description: The main city of a country where the government is based
words.example: The capital is Buenos Aires.

words.description: The number of people living in a place
words.example: The capital has a population of more than 10 million people.

words.description: The main official language of China
words.example: In China they speak Mandarin (and Cantonese).

words.description: The official language of Israel
words.example: In Israel they speak Hebrew.

words.description: One of the large land masses on Earth
words.example: The continents in the world are Europe, Africa, Asia, North America, South America, Australia and Antarctica.

words.description: A region including countries like the UAE and Saudi Arabia
words.example: The Middle East includes United Arab Emirates and Saudi Arabia.

words.description: A region including countries like Thailand and Japan
words.example: The Far East includes Thailand and Japan.

words.description: A region including countries like Jamaica and Barbados
words.example: The Caribbean includes Jamaica and Barbados.

words.description: The northern European region including Sweden, Norway, Denmark
words.example: Scandinavia includes Sweden, Norway, Denmark and Finland.

words.description: Used with 'the' to refer to a nationality group as a whole
words.example: The British / The French are...

words.description: Nationalities ending in -ian/-ian that take 's' for plural, no 'the' needed
words.example: Brazilians/Russians are...

units.title: The physical world
units.topic: Geographic features and facts about the physical world

words.description: The top or outside part of something
words.example: Two thirds of the surface of the Earth is covered in water.

words.description: The planet we live on
words.example: Two thirds of the surface of the Earth is covered in water.

words.description: Having a layer of something over the surface
words.example: The surface was covered in water; the ground was covered with snow.

words.description: How hot or cold something is
words.example: Temperatures of over 57 degrees Celsius have been recorded.

words.description: A unit of measurement for temperature
words.example: 57¬įC (57 degrees Celsius).

words.description: A large natural hole in the side of a hill or under the ground
words.example: The Krubera-Voronja Cave is the deepest cave in the world.

words.description: An artificial waterway for boats or irrigation
words.example: The Beijing-Hangzhou Grand Canal is the longest canal in the world.

words.description: A place where water falls from a height, e.g. over a cliff
words.example: The highest waterfall in the world is the Angel Falls.

words.description: A dense tropical forest with very high rainfall
words.example: The Amazon Rainforest is the largest rainforest in the world.

words.description: A very large area of sea
words.example: The Pacific is the largest ocean in the world.

words.description: To be made or formed from particular parts
words.example: Switzerland consists of three main geographical regions.

words.description: An area within a country or the world
words.example: Switzerland consists of three main geographical regions.

words.description: A big difference between two things
words.example: Switzerland is a land of contrasts.

words.description: The appearance of an area of land
words.example: Switzerland has completely different landscapes.

words.description: The general weather conditions of a place over a long period
words.example: The climate can change within a very short distance.

words.description: The amount of space between two places
words.example: The distance between the two is just 70 kilometres.

words.description: The four main directions of the compass
words.example: Ascona in the south has an almost Mediterranean climate.

words.description: The intermediate compass directions
words.example: The wind is coming from the north-west.

units.title: Weather
units.topic: Weather conditions, rain, storms and temperature

words.description: Having thick fog; difficult to see far
words.example: It was very foggy this morning.

words.description: Having a lot of clouds in the sky
words.example: It was very cloudy this morning, but the sun came out after lunch.

words.description: Covered in ice; very cold
words.example: The roads were icy this morning.

words.description: When the sun appears after being hidden by clouds
words.example: It was cloudy, but the sun came out after lunch.

words.description: Very dense fog that makes it hard to see
words.example: The accident happened in thick fog.

words.description: A large amount of rain falling
words.example: We had some heavy rain at the weekend.

words.description: A small amount of rain falling gently
words.example: Light rain is the opposite of heavy rain.

words.description: Wind blowing with a lot of force
words.example: There was a strong wind when we were on the boat.

words.description: When wind has caused movement of something
words.example: The wind has blown all the apples off the tree.

words.description: A sky with no clouds
words.example: The sky was clear by lunchtime. [no clouds]

words.description: To a very great degree; very
words.example: It's been extremely cold today.

words.description: To rain very heavily
words.example: It poured with rain this afternoon.

words.description: A short period of light or heavy rain
words.example: We had a couple of heavy/light showers this morning.

words.description: Heavy rain with strong winds
words.example: A storm is heavy rain with strong winds.

words.description: A storm with thunder and lightning
words.example: A period of hot weather sometimes ends with a thunderstorm.

words.description: When the air feels very warm and wet
words.example: First it becomes very humid.

words.description: The loud noise heard during a storm
words.example: You hear thunder and see lightning.

words.description: A bright flash of light in the sky during a storm
words.example: You hear thunder and see lightning.

words.description: Extremely hot
words.example: Boiling means very hot.

words.description: Having a pleasant, moderate heat
words.example: It's quite warm today.

words.description: Slightly cold, either pleasantly or unpleasantly
words.example: We've had a cool summer. / The water in the pool was lovely and cool.

words.description: Unpleasantly cold
words.example: It's a bit chilly today, take a jacket.

words.description: Extremely cold; below freezing point
words.example: Freezing means very cold.

words.description: Not as cold as usual; pleasantly moderate
words.example: It's been a mild winter.

units.title: Animals and insects
units.topic: Pets, farm animals, wild animals, insects and sea creatures

words.description: To have animals living with you at home
words.example: In the UK, many people keep pets.

words.description: A container with bars for keeping birds or small animals
words.example: Parrots are usually in a cage.

words.description: A very small rodent. Singular: mouse, plural: mice
words.example: Children sometimes keep mice (singular: mouse).

words.description: A small furry animal with long ears
words.example: Children sometimes keep rabbits.

words.description: A small amphibian with smooth skin that lives near water
words.example: Some people keep more unusual pets, e.g. frogs, snakes and spiders.

words.description: A long reptile with no legs
words.example: Some people keep snakes as pets.

words.description: A small eight-legged creature that spins webs
words.example: Some people keep spiders as pets.

words.description: A farm animal similar to a small horse with long ears
words.example: Farms in the UK may have donkeys.

words.description: A farm animal kept for milk and meat
words.example: Farms in the UK may have goats.

words.description: An adult male cow
words.example: Farms in the UK may have a bull.

words.description: An animal that lives naturally in the wild, not domesticated
words.example: The pictures show a number of wild animals.

words.description: Living freely in their natural environment
words.example: You may see these animals in the wild.

words.description: A place where wild animals are kept for public display
words.example: You will probably see them in a zoo.

words.description: Not often seen or found
words.example: Some animals, for example tigers, are now quite rare.

words.description: To keep safe from harm
words.example: It is important that we protect these endangered animals.

words.description: At risk of becoming extinct
words.example: We protect these endangered animals.

words.description: A small to medium-sized primate that climbs trees
words.example: Monkey is a wild animal.

words.description: A large desert animal with one or two humps
words.example: A camel can survive in the desert without water for a long time.

words.description: A large wild cat with a spotted coat
words.example: A leopard is a wild animal with spots.

words.description: A large heavy wild mammal with thick fur
words.example: A bear is a large wild animal.

words.description: The long nose of an elephant
words.example: The elephant uses its trunk to drink water.

words.description: A living thing, especially an animal
words.example: Many different creatures live in the sea.

words.description: A very large sea mammal
words.example: A whale is the largest sea creature.

words.description: An intelligent sea mammal known for jumping out of water
words.example: Dolphins are very intelligent sea creatures.

words.description: A large predatory fish with sharp teeth
words.example: Some sharks are dangerous to humans.

words.description: A sea creature with eight arms
words.example: An octopus has eight arms.

units.title: The body and movement
units.topic: Parts of the body and movements involving mouth, face and head

words.description: The part of the face above the eyebrows
words.example: She had a headache and rubbed her forehead.

words.description: The side of the face below the eyes
words.example: The baby had round, pink cheeks.

words.description: The soft edges around the mouth opening
words.example: Drink water if your lips feel dry.

words.description: The part of the face below the mouth
words.example: She rested her chin on her hand.

words.description: The chest area of a woman's body
words.example: Breast is a part of the female chest.

words.description: The joint in the middle of the arm
words.example: I bumped my elbow on the table.

words.description: The narrow part of the body between chest and hips
words.example: She has a very slim waist.

words.description: The area between the waist and top of the leg
words.example: She fell and broke her hip.

words.description: The part of the body that you sit on
words.example: He sat down on the bottom step.

words.description: The joint connecting the hand to the lower arm
words.example: She wears a watch on her wrist.

words.description: The back part of the foot
words.example: These shoes hurt my heel.

words.description: To take air into and out of the lungs
words.example: People breathe through their nose or mouth.

words.description: To take air into the lungs / to let air out of the lungs
words.example: You breathe in and out about 12-15 times a minute.

words.description: To smile in the direction of someone
words.example: They sometimes smile at people to be polite.

words.description: To laugh because something is funny
words.example: People laugh at things which are funny.

words.description: To produce tears, usually from sadness
words.example: People sometimes cry if they're very unhappy.

words.description: To move your head up and down to mean 'yes'
words.example: People in some countries nod their head to mean 'yes'.

words.description: To move your head from side to side to mean 'no'
words.example: People shake their head for 'no'.

words.description: To open your mouth wide and breathe in deeply, usually when tired
words.example: People often yawn when they're tired.

words.transcription: / Ée…™k h√¶ndz w…™√į ňąs Ćmw Ćn/
words.description: To grasp and shake someone's hand as a greeting
words.example: They shook hands with each other when they met.

words.description: To cross your arms over your chest
words.example: He folded his arms and waited.

words.description: To move your hand from side to side as a greeting or farewell
words.example: She waved to her friend across the street.

words.description: To clear your nose by blowing air through it into a tissue
words.example: She blew her nose into a tissue.

words.description: To arrange your hair using a comb
words.example: She combs her hair every morning.

units.title: Describing appearance
units.topic: Words for describing beauty, size, hair and physical appearance

words.description: The way someone or something looks
words.example: Your appearance is the way you look.

words.description: Pleasing in appearance
words.example: Women can be attractive or good-looking.

words.description: Nice to look at; attractive
words.example: Women can be attractive or good-looking.

words.description: Attractive, especially used for a girl
words.example: We often use pretty to describe a girl.

words.description: Extremely beautiful or attractive
words.example: We use gorgeous for women who are very attractive.

words.description: Attractive, especially used for men
words.example: Liam has become quite handsome.

words.description: Not special or different; a polite alternative to 'ugly'
words.example: Ordinary [not special or different] is more polite than ugly.

words.description: How tall or short a person is
words.example: We can talk about a person's height.

words.description: How heavy a person is
words.example: We can talk about their weight.

words.description: Used to indicate a number is not exact
words.example: I'm roughly one metre eighty (tall).

words.description: Neither tall nor short
words.example: If someone is not tall or short, you can describe them as medium height.

words.description: Typical; similar to most other people
words.example: If a person is very similar to most other people in height and weight, you can say they are average.

words.description: Having light yellow coloured hair
words.example: His hair is blonde (or blond).

words.description: Light coloured hair, lighter than brown
words.example: Her hair colour is fair.

words.description: Hair with gentle curves; between straight and curly
words.example: Her hair is wavy, not straight or curly.

words.description: Hair with tight curls
words.example: Her hair is curly.

words.description: A question asking someone to describe a person's appearance
words.example: What does Sophia's boyfriend look like?

words.description: Quite tall; using the suffix -ish meaning 'quite'
words.example: He's tallish, but he's got broad shoulders.

words.description: Wide shoulders; the opposite of narrow shoulders
words.example: He's got broad shoulders. [wide; opposite narrow]

words.description: Strong, healthy and good at sports
words.example: He looks very athletic.

words.description: Clean, tidy and stylish in appearance
words.example: Is he quite smart? [clean, tidy and stylish]

words.description: To wear nice, fashionable clothes
words.example: He dresses quite well. [the clothes he wears are quite nice]
